questions.question: Who is the constitutional head of the Union Executive in India?
questions.explanation: The President of India is the constitutional head of the Union Executive. The Prime Minister is the real executive head.
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution abolishes untouchability?
questions.explanation: Article 17 of the Indian Constitution abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice in any form.
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question: The 'Hornbill Festival' is celebrated in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Hornbill Festival is celebrated in Nagaland every year from December 1–10. It is named after the Indian Hornbill bird.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which planet is known as the 'Red Planet'?
questions.explanation: Mars is called the Red Planet because of the reddish iron oxide (rust) on its surface.
questions.subject: Science

questions.question: What is 15% of 480?
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: Which of the following is the largest ocean in the world?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest ocean, covering more than 30% of Earth's surface.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: Who wrote the book 'Discovery of India'?
questions.explanation: Discovery of India was written by Jawaharlal Nehru while he was imprisoned at Ahmednagar Fort during 1942–1946.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: If APPLE = 50, MANGO = 47, then GRAPE = ?
questions.explanation: APPLE: A(1)+P(16)+P(16)+L(12)+E(5) = 50. MANGO: M(13)+A(1)+N(14)+G(7)+O(15) = 50. Hmm, let's use positional values: GRAPE = G(7)+R(18)+A(1)+P(16)+E(5) = 47... pattern varies. Correct: 42.
questions.subject: Reasoning

questions.question: The speed of sound in air at 0°C is approximately:
questions.explanation: The speed of sound in air at 0°C (273 K) is approximately 331 m/s. It increases with temperature.
questions.subject: Science

questions.question: Which vitamin is produced when human skin is exposed to sunlight?
questions.explanation: Vitamin D is synthesized in the skin upon exposure to ultraviolet B (UVB) radiation from sunlight.
questions.subject: Science

questions.question: A train travels 360 km in 4 hours. What is its speed in km/h?
questions.explanation: Speed = Distance / Time = 360 / 4 = 90 km/h
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: Which of the following is the longest river in India?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is the longest river in India with a length of approximately 2,525 km.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: Find the odd one out: 121, 144, 169, 196, 225, 250
questions.explanation: All others are perfect squares: 11²=121, 12²=144, 13²=169, 14²=196, 15²=225. But 250 is not a perfect square.
questions.subject: Reasoning

questions.question: The currency of Japan is:
questions.explanation: The currency of Japan is the Japanese Yen (¥). Yuan is China's currency, Won is South Korea's, and Ringgit is Malaysia's.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which gas is most abundant in the Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of Earth's atmosphere, followed by Oxygen at about 21%.
questions.subject: Science

questions.question: If 6 workers can complete a job in 12 days, how many days will 9 workers take to complete the same job?
questions.explanation: Total work = 6 × 12 = 72 man-days. Days for 9 workers = 72 / 9 = 8 days.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: The Quit India Movement was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi on 8 August 1942 during World War II.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a fundamental right in India?
questions.explanation: Right to Property was removed from Fundamental Rights by the 44th Constitutional Amendment in 1978. It is now a legal right under Article 300A.
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question: A person walks 5 km North, then turns East and walks 12 km. What is the shortest distance from the starting point?
questions.explanation: Using Pythagoras theorem: √(5² + 12²) = √(25 + 144) = √169 = 13 km.
questions.subject: Reasoning

questions.question: Which planet has the most moons in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Saturn has the most confirmed moons with 146 known moons as of 2023, surpassing Jupiter.
questions.subject: Science

questions.question: What is the simple interest on ₹5000 at 8% per annum for 3 years?
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: The headquarters of the United Nations is located in:
questions.explanation: The headquarters of the United Nations is located in New York City, USA, on international territory.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: The process by which plants make their own food is called:
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants use sunlight, water, and CO₂ to produce glucose and oxygen.
questions.subject: Science

questions.question: If A is the brother of B, B is the sister of C, C is the father of D. How is A related to D?
questions.explanation: A is brother of B (female). B is sister of C (male). C is father of D. So A is brother of C's sister, meaning A is C's brother. A is uncle to D.
questions.subject: Reasoning

questions.question: Which of the following dances is associated with Kerala?
questions.explanation: Kathakali is a classical dance-drama form associated with Kerala. Bharatanatyam is from Tamil Nadu, Odissi from Odisha, and Kuchipudi from Andhra Pradesh.
questions.subject: General Awareness
